_metadata.purpose: Provide reproducible demo data for Stages 1-4 grading

users.presentation_notes: Minimal health conditions; shows baseline risk scoring
Respiratory sensitivities (asthma=3, allergies=2); demonstrates personalized scoring
Multiple health conditions; demonstrates complex scoring, smart alert prioritization, map overlays
Admin user for system-level demonstrations; elderly sensitivity for forecast advice

alerts.title: Excessive Heat Warning
alerts.description: Dangerously hot conditions expected. High heat index values up to 115 degrees expected. Stay indoors and keep level of exertion to a minimum.
alerts.presentation_notes: Baseline weather alert; visible on Dashboard and Alerts page

alerts.title: Unhealthy Air Quality Alert
alerts.description: Air quality is unhealthy. The general public should avoid outdoor activities. Sensitive groups should remain indoors.
alerts.presentation_notes: High severity alert; impacts risk scoring for respiratory users

alerts.title: Wildfire Activity Detected
alerts.description: Thermal anomaly detected in satellite imagery. Possible wildfire or controlled burn. Stay alert and follow local evacuation orders.
alerts.presentation_notes: Wildfire alert with geospatial data; shown on interactive map with distance decay

alerts.title: Minor Earthquake Detected
alerts.description: A magnitude 3.1 earthquake was recorded. No significant damage expected.
alerts.presentation_notes: Seismic data; demonstrates diverse alert types on map overlay

alerts.title: Elevated Pollution Levels
alerts.description: Industrial emissions and vehicle exhaust have elevated pollution levels. Consider reducing outdoor activities.
alerts.presentation_notes: Pollution alert; impacts heart disease and COPD sensitivities in risk scoring

alerts.title: Wind Advisory
alerts.description: Strong winds expected. Gusts up to 45 mph. Unsecured objects may be blown away or damaged.
alerts.presentation_notes: Regional weather alert; demonstrates map filtering by alert type

alerts.title: Air Quality Index (Moderate)
alerts.description: Air quality is moderate. Unusually sensitive people should consider limiting prolonged outdoor exertion.
alerts.presentation_notes: Lower severity air alert; affects sensitive users (asthma, allergies)

alerts.title: Wildfire Activity - Distant
alerts.description: Thermal anomaly detected far from populated areas. Under monitoring.
alerts.presentation_notes: Distant wildfire; tests distance decay in risk scoring for map display

alerts.title: Dense Fog Advisory
alerts.description: Dense fog will reduce visibility to 1/4 mile or less. Use caution when driving.
alerts.presentation_notes: Low severity weather; shows alert filtering and range of severities

alerts.title: Air Quality Emergency
alerts.description: Air quality is hazardous. Everyone should avoid outdoor activities. Seek shelter indoors.
alerts.presentation_notes: High severity pollution; demonstrates severe risk scoring and triggers forecast warnings

alerts.title: Moderate Earthquake
alerts.description: A magnitude 4.2 earthquake was recorded. Minor damage possible in affected areas.
alerts.presentation_notes: Moderate seismic event; generates risk alert and appears on map

alerts.title: Flash Flood Warning
alerts.description: Flash flooding is expected. Move to higher ground immediately. Do not attempt to cross flooded roads.
alerts.presentation_notes: High severity weather emergency; demonstrates urgent alert prioritization

alerts.title: Major Wildfire Risk
alerts.description: Significant thermal anomalies detected. Fire risk is elevated. Prepare for evacuation.
alerts.presentation_notes: High severity wildfire; demonstrates major alert, impacts risk scoring heavily, shown prominently on map

alerts.title: Air Quality Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups
alerts.description: Air quality is unhealthy for sensitive groups. Members of sensitive groups should reduce prolonged or heavy outdoor exertion.
alerts.presentation_notes: Sensitive group alert; demonstrates personalized risk filtering based on user health conditions

alerts.title: Heat Advisory
alerts.description: Heat index values up to 103 expected. It is important to drink plenty of fluids, stay in an air-conditioned room, and check up on relatives and friends.
alerts.presentation_notes: Medium severity heat alert; affects elderly and children health sensitivities

summaries.title: Daily Environmental Summary - April 10, 2026
summaries.content:
# Daily Environmental Risk Summary

## Overview
Los Angeles experienced significant environmental challenges on April 10, 2026. Multiple weather alerts, poor air quality, and seismic activity combined to create elevated risk conditions across the region.

## Key Alerts
- **Excessive Heat Warning**: High temperatures with heat index reaching 115°F
- **Unhealthy Air Quality**: Particulate matter and ozone levels exceeded safe thresholds
- **Wildfire Activity**: Thermal anomalies detected in San Gabriel Mountains
- **Flash Flood Warning**: Heavy rainfall expected in LA River Basin
- **Moderate Earthquake**: 4.2 magnitude event recorded in Santa Clarita

## Recommendations
Residents should:
1. Stay indoors during peak heat hours (2 PM - 6 PM)
2. Use air filtration and limit outdoor exposure
3. Monitor local evacuation orders
4. Secure outdoor fixtures due to wind advisory
5. Avoid flooded roadways

## Regional Impact
High air quality and heat index scores suggest elevated health risks for sensitive populations (asthma, elderly, children) and those with respiratory conditions.
summaries.presentation_notes: Shows AI-generated summary of multiple alerts; demonstrates backend summarization capability

summaries.title: Critical Air Quality Alert Summary
summaries.content:
# Critical Air Quality Event

## Alert Details
An air quality emergency was declared at 1:00 PM on April 10, 2026. Air Quality Index (AQI) reached 310 in downtown Los Angeles, well into the hazardous range.

## Health Impact
**Everyone should avoid outdoor activities.** The hazardous AQI indicates a health alert where the general population is at increased risk.

## Affected Populations
- Individuals with asthma or COPD are at particularly high risk
- Elderly residents should remain indoors
- Children should stay in air-conditioned environments
- Outdoor workers should take breaks in filtered environments

## Duration
The alert is expected to clear by 7:00 PM. However, residual effects may persist into the evening.

## Response
- Activate air purifiers
- Close windows and doors
- Limit strenuous outdoor activity
- Monitor air quality app for real-time updates
summaries.presentation_notes: Demonstrates targeted risk alert summary; shows personalized health impact messaging
